steps: Find a taxi or steal one.|Press the horn button to start taxi mode.|Pick up passengers (blue blip on map).|Deliver them to the destination quickly for a tip bonus.|Complete 50 fares in a row for the taxi unlock bonus.
tips: Use the cinematic camera during drives — passengers tip more for smooth driving. Avoid collisions to keep the tip multiplier up.
trivia: Taxi missions have been in every GTA since GTA3. In Vice City (2002) you unlocked the Zebra Cab after 100 fares.

steps: Steal any police car.|Press the horn to activate vigilante mode.|Chase and eliminate the target criminals.|Each level adds more targets. Survive to level 12 for the armor bonus.
tips: The police car's sirens clear traffic ahead of you — use them on busy streets. Helicopters make vigilante much easier.
trivia: The original Vice City vigilante rewarded you with 150 armor after level 12 — expect a similar bonus in GTA6.

steps: Steal an ambulance.|Activate paramedic mode (horn button).|Pick up patients and deliver them to the hospital.|Be careful — patients die if you crash too hard. Complete all 12 levels for the health bonus.
tips: Plan your route before picking up patients. The hardest part is the time limit on later levels with more patients.
trivia: Paramedic level 12 is infamous among GTA veterans — it takes 45+ minutes of flawless driving. Save before attempting.

steps: Steal a fire truck.|Activate firefighter mode.|Drive to the burning vehicle and use the water cannon to extinguish it.|Put out all fires in the area to complete the level. Fireproof bonus at level 12.
tips: The water cannon has surprising range — you don't need to be right next to the fire. Stay back and spray.
trivia: Completing firefighter level 12 makes you fireproof in every GTA game. Essential for the flamethrower missions later.

steps: Find a race marker (checkered flag icon).|Pay the entry fee and start the race.|Beat the other racers to the finish line.|Win races to unlock faster cars and higher-stakes events.
tips: Use a car with high acceleration for city races (tight corners) and high top speed for highway races. The Sanchez dirt bike dominates off-road races.
trivia: Street racing is GTA6's version of GTA5's street race side activity. The best racers in Vice City hang out at the Vice Beach parking lot at midnight.

steps: Find a ringing payphone (random spawn).|Answer the call to receive the contract.|Eliminate the target using the specified method.|Some contracts require specific weapons or accident kills for bonus pay.
tips: Read the contract details carefully — using the wrong weapon halves your pay. Sniping from rooftops is the safest approach for most targets.
trivia: Payphone assassinations first appeared in GTA3 and returned in GTA Online's "The Contract" update. GTA6 brings them back with more variety.

steps: Steal or buy a boat.|Find the courier icon at any dock.|Pick up the package and deliver it across the water.|Avoid coast guard patrols — they'll chase you if you get too close.
tips: The Squalo is the best boat for courier work — fast and stable. Stay in open water rather than narrow channels to avoid patrol boats.
trivia: Vice City's massive coastline and multiple islands make boat missions a natural fit. The original Vice City had boat checkpoint missions that were fan favorites.
